About

Research & Background

I am a Ph.D. researcher at the Department of Condensed Matter Physics (DCMP), Tata Institute of Fundamental Research (TIFR), Mumbai, working under the supervision of Prof. Shankar Ghosh. My thesis investigates non-equilibrium systems under geometric and informational constraints.

My work spans granular physics, active matter, and stochastic processes — using hands-on tabletop experiments and computational tools to uncover universal principles. From understanding how granular arches form and collapse in silos, to building robot swarms that exhibit emergent collective behavior, I aim to bridge physics and engineering.

Prior to my Ph.D., I completed an M.Sc. in Physics at TIFR Hyderabad, and a B.E. in Mechanical Engineering from Jadavpur University, Kolkata — a background that uniquely positions me at the interface of theoretical physics and experimental design.

Core Research Areas

Physics at the Edge of Equilibrium

Two interlocking themes define my doctoral work: understanding how disordered granular matter clogs, flows, and yields — and how autonomous agents collectively navigate noisy, constrained environments. Both sit at the rich frontier where geometry, statistics, and dynamics intersect.

3D silo · PRL 2025
Research Area I
Granular Matter & Clogging
How do arches form at a silo outlet — and why does a well-placed obstruction paradoxically free a clogged flow? Using 3D glass-bead experiments, high-speed imaging, and flexible-string interventions, I probe the universal geometry of clogging and develop simple physical strategies to restore flow. This work led to a PRL publication and a patent filing.
Granular physics Arch formation High-speed imaging Clog mitigation PRL 2025
40-agent platform · TIFR
AprilTag localization · peer communication
Research Area II
Active Matter & Robot Swarms
A 40-agent custom-built swarm platform where each robot localizes via an overhead AprilTag camera and communicates locally to produce emergent collective motion. Alongside swarm experiments, I study optimal navigation strategies for differential-drive robots traversing disordered, noisy terrain — connecting stochastic processes, information theory, and active matter physics.
Active matter Swarm robotics AprilTag localization Stochastic navigation arXiv:2512.20336
arXiv:2512.20336
diff-drive robot · disordered glass-bead terrain
Sub-project · Navigation
Optimal Navigation in Noisy Environments
How much noise can a navigator tolerate before it must adopt a fundamentally different strategy? A differential-drive robot traverses a disordered glass-bead terrain toward a target, balancing directional commitment against the randomness imposed by the environment. The transition between regimes reveals deep connections to information-theoretic limits.
Stochastic processes Navigation theory Information theory Diff-drive robots
In preparation
arch clogged — rod approaching
Sub-project · Clog Release
Releasing a Clog Using a Rod
A slender rod inserted through a clogged granular hopper physically disrupts the load-bearing arch, restoring flow. This deceptively simple intervention reveals rich nonlinear dynamics: the threshold force, the arch geometry at failure, and the subsequent flow statistics all encode information about the frustrated force network underlying the clog.
Granular physics Arch disruption Force networks Flow restoration

Research Projects

Selected Work

🤖
40-Agent Swarm Robot Platform
Built a 40-agent swarm robot platform from scratch to study emergent collective motion. Custom-built platform enables investigation of how simple local interactions give rise to complex group behaviors — analogous to bird flocks and fish schools.
RoboticsActive MatterEmbedded CPCB Design
⚗️
Granular Clog Mitigation Experiments
Designed tabletop experiments demonstrating simple, intuitive clog mitigation strategies — including an obstruction paradoxically facilitating flow, and a flexible string suppressing clogs. Led to a PRL publication and a patent filing.
Granular PhysicsExperiment DesignHigh-speed Imaging
🎈
Weather Balloon Instrumentation System
Developing a multi-sensor instrumentation system for aerosol and atmospheric property measurements on a weather balloon platform — combining embedded systems, sensor fusion, and data acquisition.
InstrumentationRaspberry PiAtmospheric Physics
📐
Optimal Navigation in Noisy Environments
Investigated strategies for optimal navigation when environmental information is incomplete or noisy — connecting stochastic processes, information theory, and active matter physics. Currently under review (arXiv:2512.20336).
Stochastic ProcessesNavigationInformation Theory
